recommendations: add and run a dedicated MCP test suite in CI (smoke + contract tests for all exposed endpoints)
deduplicate and normalize endpoint names/signatures in the adapter (e.g.
introduce strict Pydantic request/response schemas with clear error models for every tool
pin and validate compatibility matrix across Python 3.11–3.13 and key deps (xarray/dask/fsspec/zarr)
add requirements/dev lock files (or uv/poetry lock) for reproducible MCP builds
harden I/O paths with explicit validation/sandboxing and safer cache clearing behavior
add structured logging + tracing around heavy read paths (parse_meta_file/read_mds/get_dataset) with timing and dataset-size metadata
implement performance regression benchmarks for large MDS/LLC loads and remote HTTPStore access
add type-checking and lint gates (mypy/ruff) for mcp_output plus public xmitgcm-facing wrappers
add resilience features for remote stores (timeouts/retries/backoff/circuit-breaker)
cache metadata/index parsing results where safe to reduce repeated startup latency
expose versioned API surface for MCP tools to avoid breaking downstream clients
add security/dependency scanning in CI (pip-audit/safety + codeql)
publish minimal health and readiness endpoints (or equivalent MCP checks) for deployment monitoring

success_factors: Workflow completed end-to-end with status=success across all planned nodes (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize)
Import-based adapter strategy was feasible (import_feasibility=0.94, low intrusiveness risk), enabling rapid MCP wrapping of existing APIs
Repository structure was analyzable via zip fallback (33 files discovered) despite DeepWiki failure
Generated MCP service started and passed plugin-level health checks (healthy, stdio transport)
failure_reasons: DeepWiki analysis failed, reducing external semantic enrichment but not blocking generation
Original project test stage reported passed=false with zero execution evidence (execution_time=0, no test files), indicating test validation gap rather than runtime failure
Generation metadata inconsistency (tool_endpoints=0 vs discovered endpoint list) suggests reporting/instrumentation defect

node_performance.download_time: Completed successfully; exact per-node timing not provided. Zip fallback import indicates resilient but potentially slower retrieval path.
node_performance.analysis_time: Completed successfully with structural/dependency/risk outputs; moderate analysis depth achieved without LLM/DeepWiki call utilization.
node_performance.generation_time: Completed successfully; MCP scaffold and adapter files produced, but code-size metrics recorded as zero indicate telemetry inaccuracies.
node_performance.test_time: MCP plugin smoke health passed; original project tests were effectively not executed (0s), so validation quality is incomplete.
